text: laparoscopic duodenal jejunostomy for superior mesenteric artery syndrome. Sixty-two-year-old female presented with a complicated history, including anti-reflux surgery. She had poor oral intake and persistent weight loss after surgery. On her initial workup, she was diagnosed with delayed gastric emptying. Laparoscopic pyloroplasty resolved her symptoms partially.

Further workup showed normal gastric emptying, but an upper GI demonstrating bowel obstruction at junction of third and fourth portion of duodenum, attributed to superior mesenteric artery syndrome. Upper GI endoscopy confirmed extraluminal compression in this area.

In the operating room, the surgeon performs a laparoscopic duodenal jejunostomy. After lysing adhesions, the transverse colon and omentum are retracted cephalad to expose the ligament of Treitz. The dilated third portion of the duodenum is visible to the right of the middle colic vessels. A window is created in the transverse mesocolon, and blunt dissection frees the duodenum from retroperitoneal and mesocolic attachments in a Cattell-Braasch maneuver. The jejunum is drawn 40 centimeters from the ligament of Treitz and placed adjacent to the duodenum without tension. A stay suture is placed, and entotomies are created with hook electrocautery. A side-to-side anastomosis is formed using a 60mm laparoscopic linear stapling device with a 2.5mm staple load. The common enterotomy is closed with running IIO absorbable suture in a single layer.

The surgeon places a reinforcement suture at the apex of the anastomosis to minimize strain. The efferent limb of the jejunum is sutured to the retroperitoneum with permanent sutures to prevent kinking. An intraoperative endoscopy is performed to evaluate the anastomosis, and an air leak test is conducted, showing no leaks.

The patient did well following surgery. She was gradually advanced to a soft diet and was discharged home on post-operative day two. At her one month post-operative visit, she had gained two point three kilograms since surgery.
